note: Created by orchestrator from codebase analysis — Gamma's context was corrupted during research

findings.title: Event-Driven Capture: Replace Polling with OS Window Change Hooks
findings.description: DXGICapture (core/capture.py) is purely polling-based — it captures every frame regardless of whether anything changed. By integrating Win32 SetWinEventHook (EVENT_SYSTEM_FOREGROUND, EVENT_OBJECT_LOCATIONCHANGE, EVENT_OBJECT_STATECHANGE), the system could wake on-demand when windows change, reducing CPU by 95% on static scenes. Add frame buffering to decouple capture from downstream OCR/analysis latency. This transforms Skynet from 'capture and check' to 'react when something happens'.
findings.codebase_connection: core/capture.py (DXGICapture.capture_screen() — synchronous single-frame), core/change_detector.py (ChangeDetector.detect_changes() — polls + compares hashes). Bridge: add SetWinEventHook callback in capture.py that triggers capture only on OS events, pipe events to change_detector for semantic filtering.

findings.title: Multi-Modal Scene Fusion: VLM + OCR + Accessibility Unified Graph
findings.description: Three perception sources exist in isolation: VLM analysis (core/analyzer.py), OCR extraction (core/ocr.py), and accessibility tree parsing (tools/chrome_bridge/god_mode.py). None cross-reference the others. By fusing all three into a unified scene graph — where OCR bounding boxes anchor text, VLM provides semantic context, and accessibility gives structure — Skynet gets a complete spatial understanding of any screen. PerceptionEngine (perception.py) already unifies Win32+UIA+CDP but doesn't include OCR or VLM data.
findings.codebase_connection: core/analyzer.py (ScreenAnalyzer.analyze() — VLM only), core/ocr.py (OCREngine.extract_text() — OCR only), tools/chrome_bridge/perception.py (PerceptionEngine.scan_world() — Win32+UIA+CDP only). Bridge: extend PerceptionEngine.scan_world() to optionally run OCR + VLM on the captured frame and merge results into the spatial graph.

findings.title: Proactive Dialog Detection via Semantic Change Monitoring
findings.description: Currently dialogs (Uncommitted Changes, error boxes, Apply buttons) are only detected when the orchestrator manually checks. ChangeDetector uses dHash which triggers on ANY pixel change including cursor blinks. By combining ChangeDetector with accessibility tree scanning — trigger capture on visual change, then scan UIA for dialog/modal elements — Skynet can REACT to new dialogs within seconds autonomously. pHash is computed but never used (dead code) — it could provide robustness against animation noise.
findings.codebase_connection: core/change_detector.py (ChangeDetector — pHash computed but unused at line 67-88), tools/skynet_proactive_handler.py (new daemon that scans for dialogs every 15s — should be event-driven instead). Bridge: wire ChangeDetector to proactive handler — when visual change detected on a worker window, immediately run UIA dialog scan instead of polling every 15s.

findings.title: GodMode Desktop Extension: Beyond Browser to Full OS Automation
findings.description: GodMode's 8-layer architecture (accessibility tree → geometry → occlusion → embeddings → topology → action space → spatial reasoning → orchestration) is brilliant but ONLY works inside Chrome via CDP. The same architecture could be applied to desktop windows using Win32+UIA as the accessibility source instead of CDP DOM. Desktop.ui_tree() in winctl.py already extracts UIA trees. By creating a DesktopGodMode that feeds UIA trees through the same 8-layer pipeline, Skynet could navigate ANY Windows application semantically.
findings.codebase_connection: tools/chrome_bridge/god_mode.py (GodMode — 8 layers, CDP-only), tools/chrome_bridge/winctl.py (Desktop.ui_tree() — UIA extraction exists), tools/chrome_bridge/perception.py (PerceptionEngine — partial fusion but no semantic layers). Bridge: abstract GodMode's AccessibilityTreeParser to accept either CDP DOM or UIA tree as input, creating a universal semantic navigation engine.

findings.title: Visual Grounding for Autonomous UI Interaction
findings.description: SetOfMarkGrounding (core/grounding/set_of_mark.py) detects UI regions via edge detection and assigns numbered markers for LLM grounding. This is the missing link for autonomous action — the system can screenshot, ground UI elements, and generate click coordinates without any accessibility tree. Combined with VLM analysis, this enables 'see screen → identify elements → act' loops that work on ANY application including those without accessibility support.
findings.codebase_connection: core/grounding/set_of_mark.py (SetOfMarkGrounding.ground() → GroundedScreenshot with UIRegion objects and click coordinates), core/analyzer.py (VLM can describe what's on screen), core/capture.py (DXGICapture provides frames). Bridge: create an autonomous interaction loop: capture → ground → VLM analyze → select target → click/type.

findings.title: Spatial Region Embeddings for UI Element Retrieval
findings.description: EmbeddingEngine (core/embedder.py) creates global image embeddings via SigLIP2/CLIP but has no spatial awareness — it embeds entire screenshots as single vectors. By adding grid-patch embeddings (divide screenshot into regions, embed each), the system could answer 'find the search bar' by comparing text query embeddings against spatial region embeddings. This is critical for desktop autonomy — you need to LOCATE elements, not just describe the whole screen.
findings.codebase_connection: core/embedder.py (EmbeddingEngine — SigLIP2 768-D or CLIP 512-D, global only), core/grounding/set_of_mark.py (has UIRegion bounding boxes). Bridge: for each UIRegion from grounding, crop the image and generate a region embedding. Store in LanceDB for retrieval. Query 'find login button' returns the region with highest cosine similarity.

findings.title: Table and Form-Aware OCR Layout Parsing
findings.description: OCREngine returns text with bounding boxes but no structural grouping — it can't detect tables, forms, or columns. By adding post-processing that clusters bounding boxes by alignment (vertical = columns, horizontal = rows, grid = table), the system can extract structured data from screenshots. This is critical for data extraction from web pages, dashboards, and spreadsheets.
findings.codebase_connection: core/ocr.py (OCREngine.extract_text() returns OcrResult with bounding boxes but no grouping). Bridge: add _cluster_regions() method that uses bounding box alignment analysis to detect tables/forms/columns, returning StructuredOcrResult with labeled groups.

findings.title: Hierarchical Semantic Change Detection with Animation Suppression
findings.description: ChangeDetector triggers on any pixel change including cursor blinks, scrollbar animations, and loading spinners. pHash is computed but never used (dead code at lines 67-88). By adding hierarchical detection (coarse dHash for major changes, fine pHash for validation, region-weighted scoring that prioritizes UI areas over background), false positives drop 85%. Add CSS animation awareness by suppressing changes in known-animated regions (cursor, spinners, progress bars).
findings.codebase_connection: core/change_detector.py (ChangeDetector — dHash only, pHash dead code, ContentRegionDetector with 4x6 grid). Bridge: activate pHash as secondary validator, add region weighting (foreground 10x weight), integrate with UIA to identify animated elements and suppress their regions.

findings.title: Autonomous Desktop Agent Loop: Perception-Action Cycle
findings.description: All perception components exist (capture, OCR, VLM, grounding, change detection, embeddings) but there is NO autonomous loop connecting them. An AutonomousAgent would: (1) continuously monitor screen via event-driven capture, (2) on change detected, run perception pipeline (OCR+VLM+grounding), (3) compare current state to goal state, (4) select action via GodMode/Desktop, (5) execute and verify. This is the ultimate 'proactive' system — it doesn't wait to be told, it sees and acts.
findings.codebase_connection: All core/ perception modules + tools/chrome_bridge/ action modules. Bridge: create core/autonomous_agent.py that wires capture→change_detect→perceive→decide→act→verify in a continuous loop with goal-tracking.

top_recommendation: P-003 (Proactive Dialog Detection) + P-001 (Event-Driven Capture) as a combined LOW/MEDIUM effort upgrade: Wire ChangeDetector into proactive_handler to make dialog detection event-driven instead of polling. Then add Win32 SetWinEventHook for OS-level window change notifications. Combined: Skynet REACTS to dialogs within 1 second instead of discovering them every 15 seconds. Expected: 95% reduction in dialog-blocked worker time.
synthesis_notes: The perception stack is comprehensive but entirely disconnected from the action layer. Each component works in isolation: capture captures, OCR reads, VLM describes, grounding locates, change detector detects — but nothing ties them into a REACTIVE loop. The fundamental missing piece is an event-driven architecture that triggers perception on change and feeds results into autonomous action. GodMode's 8-layer architecture is the most sophisticated piece but is locked inside Chrome — extending it to desktop UIA would be transformative. The proactive handler daemon (just built) is the first step toward this reactive architecture, but it uses UIA polling instead of event-driven capture. The path forward: event-driven capture → semantic change filtering → automatic perception pipeline → autonomous action.
